Hotfix: Synapse uses your configured PostgreSQL again instead of silently falling back to SQLite.

## Fixed

- **Synapse no longer silently falls back to SQLite.** A regression after v2.0.1 dropped the second `--config-path` that loads `homeserver-overrides.yaml` and brought back the inert `include_config_files` directive in `homeserver.yaml`. Synapse does not honor `include_config_files`, so the overrides (PostgreSQL, federation, TURN, registration) were dead config again and the server ran on SQLite at `/data/homeserver.db`. The overrides are now passed to Synapse via a second `--config-path` once more, so your configured database, federation, and TURN settings actually take effect. Thanks to the reporter in issue #3 for catching that the v2.0.1 fix had not actually landed on `main`.

## Changed

- **Upgrade cleanup.** On start the container strips any stale `include_config_files` block from an existing `homeserver.yaml`, so the file matches what Synapse really reads.
- **Orphaned-SQLite warning.** If a legacy `/data/homeserver.db` is found, the container now warns that Synapse will use PostgreSQL from now on and points you at `synapse_port_db` in case you need to migrate the old SQLite data first.
